stem: A circle in the xy-plane has equation (x − 3)2 + (y + 2)2 = 36. Which of the following points does NOT lie in the interior of the circle?
rationale: The circle has center (3, −2) and radius 6. A point lies in the interior exactly when its distance to (3, −2) is less than 6. For (10, 2), the distance is √((10−3)2 + (2+2)2) = √(49 + 16) = √65 ≈ 8.06, which is greater than 6, so this point is NOT in the interior. The other three points all have distance less than 6.
